pytorch.reason: compute winding number from angle sequence via cumulative sum; autograd confirms dW/d(continuous_deformation)=0 (topological invariance under smooth perturbation)

pyg.reason: not used — winding number bridge is loop/angle based; no graph message-passing required

z3.reason: UNSAT: winding_number changes continuously between integers 1 and 0 (must be integer-valued; no real value strictly between 0 and 1 can arise from continuous loop deformation)

cvc5.reason: not used — z3 covers the proof layer for this sim

sympy.reason: exact symbolic winding number integral (1/2pi)*∮ dθ for parametric circle and figure-eight; confirm integer output and verify homotopy class invariance

clifford.reason: rotor R=exp(θ/2 e12) traverses S¹; R^2=-1 at θ=2π confirms double-cover; winding number encodes how many times rotor circuit wraps the algebra

geomstats.reason: not used — winding number bridge uses angular arithmetic; geomstats Riemannian tools not required at this level

e3nn.reason: not used — winding number bridge is topological/algebraic; equivariant networks not required

rustworkx.reason: homotopy class graph — loops in same class (same winding number) are connected nodes; loops in different classes are in disconnected components; graph structure encodes π₁(S¹)=Z

xgi.reason: not used — winding number bridge is path-based; no hypergraph topology required

toponetx.reason: not used — winding number bridge uses fundamental group; cell complex homology not required at this level

gudhi.reason: persistent homology H1 of a circle (winding loops) detects the loop that generates π₁(S¹); confirms topological distinction between winding classes via Betti number

P1_pytorch_winding_number_circle_is_1.description: Pytorch: standard circle loop (0 to 2pi) has winding number = 1 (one full wrap)

P2_pytorch_winding_number_double_loop_is.description: Pytorch: double-wound circle loop (0 to 4pi) has winding number = 2

P3_pytorch_winding_number_contractible_i.description: Pytorch: constant (contractible) loop has winding number = 0

P4_pytorch_continuous_deformation_preser.description: Pytorch: small smooth perturbation of circle loop leaves winding number = 1 (topological invariance)

P5_sympy_winding_integral_unit_circle.description: Sympy: (1/2pi)*∫₀^{2pi} dθ = 1 for unit circle — winding number is integer-valued by construction

P6_clifford_rotor_double_cover.description: Clifford: R(2pi)=-1 and R(4pi)=+1 — rotor encodes Z₂ double-cover structure of SU(2)→SO(3)

P7_rustworkx_homotopy_class_graph_3_comp.description: Rustworkx: homotopy class graph has 3 connected components (W=0, W=1, W=2) — loops in different classes are disconnected

P8_gudhi_circle_betti_b1_equals_1.description: Gudhi: Rips complex on circle samples has persistent b0=1, b1=1 — one topological loop, confirms π₁(S¹)≠0

P9_pytorch_reversed_loop_winding_minus_1.description: Pytorch: reversed circle loop (clockwise) has winding number = -1 (π₁(S¹)=Z includes negatives)

N1_z3_winding_number_not_between_integer.description: Z3 UNSAT: winding number strictly between 0 and 1 is impossible — W must be integer-valued for closed loops

N2_pytorch_unclosed_path_noninteger_wind.description: Negative: unclosed path (3/4 circle) yields non-integer winding proxy ~ 0.75 — integrality requires closure

N3_sympy_reparametrization_invariance.description: Negative: winding number is invariant under reparametrization — different speed, same topological class

N4_clifford_rotor_0_neq_rotor_2pi.description: Negative: Clifford R(0)=+1 ≠ R(2pi)=-1 despite both corresponding to identity in SO(3) — double cover is real

N5_rustworkx_different_winding_not_conne.description: Negative: loops with W=0 and W=1 have no edge in homotopy graph — topologically distinct, disconnected

N6_gudhi_two_circles_b0_equals_2.description: Negative: two separate circles have persistent b0=2 (disconnected) — distinct topological classes

B1_pytorch_winding_number_3.description: Boundary: loop winding 3 times gives winding number = 3

B1_pytorch_winding_number_5.description: Boundary: loop winding 5 times gives winding number = 5

B1_pytorch_winding_number_10.description: Boundary: loop winding 10 times gives winding number = 10

B2_sympy_winding_n_1.description: Sympy boundary: n=1 winding integral gives W=1 exactly

B2_sympy_winding_n_2.description: Sympy boundary: n=2 winding integral gives W=2 exactly

B2_sympy_winding_n_3.description: Sympy boundary: n=3 winding integral gives W=3 exactly

B3_clifford_rotor_n_1_winding.description: Clifford boundary: R(n*2pi) scalar = (-1)^n = -1 for n=1

B3_clifford_rotor_n_2_winding.description: Clifford boundary: R(n*2pi) scalar = (-1)^n = 1 for n=2

B3_clifford_rotor_n_3_winding.description: Clifford boundary: R(n*2pi) scalar = (-1)^n = -1 for n=3

B3_clifford_rotor_n_4_winding.description: Clifford boundary: R(n*2pi) scalar = (-1)^n = 1 for n=4

B4_z3_integer_winding_0_is_consistent.description: Boundary z3 SAT: winding number = 0 is consistent — all integers are valid homotopy classes

B4_z3_integer_winding_1_is_consistent.description: Boundary z3 SAT: winding number = 1 is consistent — all integers are valid homotopy classes

B4_z3_integer_winding_-1_is_consistent.description: Boundary z3 SAT: winding number = -1 is consistent — all integers are valid homotopy classes

B4_z3_integer_winding_2_is_consistent.description: Boundary z3 SAT: winding number = 2 is consistent — all integers are valid homotopy classes

B5_rustworkx_z5_homotopy_graph_5_compone.description: Boundary: homotopy graph for W∈{-2,-1,0,1,2} has 5 components — encodes truncated Z

B6_gudhi_filled_disk_b1_equals_0.description: Boundary: filled disk has persistent b1=0 — contractible, winding number always 0; contrast with circle b1=1
